title: justicia tocantina subsp. andina
intro: Justicia tocantina subsp. andina, also known as the Andean water willow or simply water willow, is a plant species native to the Andean region of South America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, pain, and inflammation, due to its high concentration of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Justicia tocantina subsp. andina include reducing fever, relieving pain and inflammation, and acting as an antioxidant, which can help protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
